name: Sierra de Mijas Loop from Fuengirola
name_original: Sierra de Mijas – circular desde Fuengirola
description: Challenging 38-mile road cycling route in Sierra de Mijas from Fuengirola, offering panoramic coastal views and significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fuengirola offer a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features well-maintained coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea, providing opportunities for leisurely rides. Inland, the terrain transitions to challenging mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Mijas and Montes de Málaga, characterized by pine forests, rocky outcrops, and significant elevation gains. These areas provide varied cycling experiences, from flat coastal stretches to demanding climbs. Best road cycling routes around Fuengirola The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Mijas – circular from Fuengirola, a 38.0 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging climbs and panoramic views of the Mediterranean. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mijas – Mijas Repeater Climb loop from Los Boliches, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route offers a scenic ascent through the Sierra de Mijas. Local road cyclists also love the View of Fuengirola – Mijas loop from Fuengirola, a 46.4 miles (74.6 km) trail leading through diverse terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Fuengirola is defined by coastal paths, the Sierra de Mijas, and pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Fuengirola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuengirola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Fuengirola?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Fuengirola, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find options for various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Fuengirola?
A: Fuengirola offers a mix of terrain. You can enjoy well-maintained coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea for leisurely rides. Inland, the landscape transitions to challenging mountain ranges like the Sierra de Mijas and Montes de Málaga, characterized by pine forests, rocky outcrops, and significant elevation gains.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fuengirola?
A: The mild climate makes Fuengirola ideal for road cycling year-round. However, spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) offer particularly pleasant temperatures, making them excellent times for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or easy rides?
A: Yes, Fuengirola has several easy road cycling routes. The coastal paths and promenades, such as those along Playa de Fuengirola and Los Boliches, provide gentle rides with sea breezes. There are 7 easy routes available, perfect for those looking for a relaxed experience.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the areas surrounding Fuengirola offer rewarding climbs. The Sierra de Mijas is a popular choice, with routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932025721/sierra-de-mijas-circular-from-fuengirola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Mijas – circular from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.1 km with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. There are 19 difficult routes in total.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes in Fuengirola?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. You might pass by the historic Sohail Castle, enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Majano - La Esperanza&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Inca Botanical Garden&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mijas&lt;/a&gt; and natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarapalos Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Fuengirola?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Fuengirola are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932025721/sierra-de-mijas-circular-from-fuengirola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Mijas – circular from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32263514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Fuengirola – Mijas loop from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery and elevation.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Fuengirola?
A: The road cycling routes in Fuengirola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 5,000 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that offer shade during summer rides?
A: Yes, for rides during warmer months, consider routes that venture into the nearby mountain ranges like the Sierra de Mijas or Montes de Málaga. These areas feature fragrant pine forests and cork oak groves, which can provide welcome shade along certain sections of the trails.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes around Fuengirola pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. Coastal routes often have numerous options along the promenade, while inland routes might lead you through charming villages in the Sierra de Mijas, offering opportunities for a break.

Q: Are there specific routes known for panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Routes that ascend into the Sierra de Mijas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijas – Mijas Repeater Climb loop from Los Boliches&lt;/a&gt;, offer stunning panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean Sea and the coastline. Peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mijas&lt;/a&gt; and viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Majano - La Esperanza&lt;/a&gt; are known for their breathtaking scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling trails near Fuengirola?
A: While Fuengirola itself offers many routes, the region is part of longer networks. An easy 31 km route, part of EuroVelo 8, connects Fuengirola to Malaga. For more extensive rides, you can find routes extending towards Ronda or even Nerja, utilizing the diverse terrain of the Costa del Sol.
tips: Street parallel to the highway from Malaga - much quieter and with less traffic
closed on Mondays, free admission for retirees!

The stupa with a wonderful view of the sea is one of the top attractions. Much on the Costa del Sol is illuminated at night, so a visit at night is also worthwhile.
The stupa can be visited and there is no entry fee. There is also a cafe and a small shop. Meditations and spiritual meetings take place in the stupa.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route, covering approximately 61.1 km (38 miles) with about 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) of elevation gain, typically takes around 3 hours and 12 minutes to complete for a cyclist with a very good fitness level.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a "hard road ride" and requires a very good level of fitness due to significant elevation gains and steep sections. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Mijas circular from Fuengirola?
faqs.answer: The terrain offers a diverse experience, transitioning from well-maintained coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to the more demanding mountain ranges of the Sierra de Mijas. While mostly well-paved and suitable for road bikes, you'll encounter pine forests, rocky outcrops, and gentle mountain curves, especially approaching Mijas Pueblo.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers continuous stunning views of the coastline and towns below. You'll pass by highlights such as a View of Fuengirola, a Quiet back road with a few ups and downs, and the charming Mijas Pueblo. Other notable points include the Molino de Inca Botanical Garden and the Stupa of Enlightenment, Benalmádena.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Mijas cycling route from Fuengirola?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for cyclists aren't detailed, as the route starts and ends in Fuengirola, you would typically look for public parking within the town. Many cyclists start directly from their accommodation or a convenient public parking spot in Fuengirola to begin the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially during ascents, and winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though the region generally enjoys mild winters.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra de Mijas?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Sierra de Mijas. The roads are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Mijas – circular from Fuengirola route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered a dog-friendly activity. Cycling with a dog, especially on a challenging road route with traffic, can be unsafe for both the cyclist and the dog.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails, including the Variante GR 249.2 Puerto de los Pescadores - Alhaurín de la Torre and [CIMA ML15] Cerro del Moro * Fuengirola.

faqs.question: Are there any challenging optional climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking an extra challenge, there's an optional climb to the Mijas Repetidores (antenna point). This segment features steep gradients exceeding 10%, with short ramps reaching 15-18%, and is recommended only for those comfortable with demanding ascents and narrow roads.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mijas Pueblo, a charming white village along the route, serves as an excellent cultural landmark and a potential rest stop. You'll find cafes and restaurants there to refuel and enjoy the traditional Andalusian ambiance.

waypoints.name: Quiet back road with a few ups and downs
